en_body:
I sit before the computer desk...the tranquil keys lie waiting for the fingers to find their way home. Today white clouds conceal the sky and filter light into a mystical dream. The air is deliciously cool though noon has already declined. Cicadas are singing high and low, serenely playing with a Summer of loving-kindness. Outside, the wind frolics and trembles the tender green leaves fluttering like butterflies. Inside it is so silent that I hear clearly the computer and refrigerator humming steadily. I welcome into myself the sacredness of Life-force. Here, I think, everything is complete, whole, and sufficient, in the most relative way. The wave of consciousness drifts beneath the inward life, the ripples upon the spiritual lake are invitations of Grace from the Creator...Yes, this is already the peaceful heart and purified mind. As I forever watch for every approaching footstep of my two young children into Life, my Creator whom I privately name THOU forever upholds the totality of my being into the earthly realm.
 Suddenly within my Wisdom-Eye, I see amid the universe those spheres of fire revolving exactly within the system of Cosmic Laws like calendar pictures capturing the phenomena of the universe; then I see the blue planet Earth still embracing and nourishing ten-thousand living beings; and I see high mountains dressed in white snow with caps of cloud; and I see the poetic tropical islands, coconut trees letting down their hair beside white sands, formed from volcanoes thrusting up the secret earth; and I see a sunlit road embracing Immensity between sea and sky: the gossamer thread of horizon dividing the blue of ocean from the celestial blue into two yet still One...I see the wind filtering earth and heaven; I see the generous sun marinating every atom forever moving in its cyclic dance, feeding the air for living beings to breathe; I see Light as the Invisible that human eyes recognize in the miracle of seeing; I see all creatures joyfully eating, sleeping, playing in unawareness; I see humanity and I see myself among humanity: I feel so deeply the Love of the Creator within me, teaching me how to love. To recognize that imperfection comes from my own limitations is to know how to pray unto the Creator, to receive Grace as the wisdom of loving rightly: to compassionate others through awakening self and awakening others within myself: and to learn what true Humility is, and call GOD home within me.
 I sit here in a curtained room, keeping the house cool to avoid wasting the common energy-source of Earth, before a computer created by the imaginative mind of my fellow beings: I have seen thus throughout all the world: therefore no boundary remains between the concrete material world and the spiritual world under the subtle vision of the Wisdom-Eye. It is the longing to learn to love as generously as Life that has given me deep understanding of the words of the great author Antoine de Saint-Exupéry: "It is only with the heart that one can see rightly. What is essential is invisible to the eye." ("Only with the heart can we see rightly. What is essential is invisible to the eyes.")
note: The closing quotation is from Antoine de Saint-Exupéry's The Little Prince.
